relations.note: Coral restriction specializes the tertiary-as-accent principle.
Chart color usage applies the blues/golds dominance in the chart context.

quotes: Apply the palette in the **primary > secondary > tertiary ratio** shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.

rules.intent: Preserve the intended visual weighting of the brand palette.
rules.rule_text: Apply the palette in the primary > secondary > tertiary ratio shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.

quotes: **Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds
**Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).

rules.intent: Ensure legible headline/body color on both light and dark surfaces.
rules.rule_text: Headlines/headings use Brand Blue (headline.color = #00529b), switching to White on dark/gradient backgrounds. Body text uses Graphite (body.color = #212121) on light backgrounds and White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height per §1.2).

quotes: **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**
**Charts/about:blank** Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.

rules.intent: Keep chart color semantics consistent and preserve emphasis for key figures.
rules.rule_text: In charts, Brand Blue (#00529b) + Sky Blue (#358CCB) dominate; Sunshine/Gold is strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures only; gray is used for placebo.

quotes: Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).
**Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).

rules.intent: Standardize callout surface fills by callout type.
rules.rule_text: Callout surfaces use Light Blue (#E6F0F9) at 70% tint for data callouts and Golden Sand (#FEF1C8) at 30% tint for text-heavy callouts.

effect.reserved_for: sparing tertiary accent only — never primary CTAs, headlines, or data
effect.subject: Coral (#F26A38)

quotes: sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data
**Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. **[GENERAL]:** avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

rules.intent: Prevent Coral from being used as a primary color or in data/CTAs.
rules.rule_text: Coral (#F26A38) is a sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. [GENERAL]: avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

quotes: Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.
**Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.

rules.intent: Ensure links are visibly styled and legible on any background.
rules.rule_text: [GENERAL] Links are Brand Blue (body.link.color = #00529b), underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark backgrounds.

quotes: 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances
The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).

rules.intent: Protect the trademark logo mark's fixed opacity treatment.
rules.rule_text: The logo icon's 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a (logo.mark.opacity = 0.7) must be maintained in all instances — in the logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic.
